title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White awarded his first NBA All-Defensive First Team selection for exceptional defense in 2025-26.
White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, leading guards with 552 contested shots and 1.3 blocks per game.
White becomes the 11th Celtic named to an All-Defensive First Team, helping Boston finish second in the East.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed' starring Tatiana Maslany is now available on Apple TV.
'Glory' is leaving Netflix on June 1; catch it while it's still streaming.
'Arco', an Oscar-nominated animated film, is currently streaming on Hulu.

title: Chad Tracy provides injury updates for Garrett Crochet, Roman Anthony
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Garrett Crochet will start a throwing session this weekend, aiming for live batting practice next week.
Roman Anthony is experiencing pain tolerance issues after swinging, impacting his return from the 10-day IL.
Trevor Story's sports hernia surgery will sideline him for approximately 6-10 weeks, per Chad Tracy.

title: Sam Kennedy says Red Sox looking to add bat ‘earlier than ever’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox president Sam Kennedy reveals early discussions about acquiring a hitter to boost the team's offense.
The team struggles offensively with 181 runs and 36 home runs, contributing to a 22-27 record so far.
Injuries and player departures have strained the lineup, prompting urgent roster improvement talks by chief officer Craig Breslow.

title: Mock draft predicts Red Sox will select Bishop Feehan star in first round
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: MLB.com's Jonathan Mayo predicts Red Sox will draft Brody Bumila 20th overall from Bishop Feehan.
Bumila, a 6-foot-9 left-handed pitcher, has impressed with his fastball-heavy style and recent game performance.
Despite some concerns, experts agree Bumila's potential makes him a candidate for a higher pick.

title: Red Sox’ Trevor Story undergoes sports hernia surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Trevor Story underwent sports hernia surgery and will miss about 6-10 weeks, affecting Red Sox's roster.
This is Story's third surgery with the Red Sox since signing in 2022, following shoulder and elbow surgeries.
Backup players Isaiah Kiner-Falefa and Andruw Monasterio are filling in at shortstop during Story's absence.

title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design six decades after leaving art school.
Wolf left the Boston Museum School of Fine Arts to form the J. Geils Band and pursue a music career.
He continues to work on a new album, a potential book, and reflects on his lifelong passion for painting.

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh joins Boston 25 as a sports media journalist, a new role at the station.
Walsh previously worked as a sports anchor in Nashville and interned at NESN.
Originally from Medford, Walsh's career includes positions in Winston-Salem, Burlington, and Syracuse.

title: Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Olivia’s Kitchen owner Simona Sbano said a viral harsh reply to a review was not authorized by ownership.
The controversial reply criticized Somerville tap water quality and customer reading comprehension skills.
Olivia's Kitchen will now serve tap water on request; previous issues were due to a visual cloudiness concern.

title: With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council failed to reject Mayor Michelle Wu's budget with a split 6-6 vote.
Councilor Brian Worrell advocated for rejecting the budget to push for increased spending.
Mayor Wu refused to increase spending despite council's consideration to amend budget items.
